editableNewDialogue.text:
Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and English grammar for this sentence:
“Yesterday I go to the store and buy a necklace, but it is too expensive, so I return it.”
Use simple words.
“Yesterday, I went to the store and bought a necklace, but it was too expensive, so I returned it.”
Make it a bit longer with one more sentence.
“Yesterday, I went to the store and bought a necklace, but it was too expensive, so I returned it. I decided to look for a better price at another shop.”

Fix this one too:
“Some people believe a four-leaf clover bring good luck, and they keep it for protect them.”
“Some people believe a four-leaf clover brings good luck, and they keep it to protect themselves.”

One more:
“In 1889, the Eiffel Tower build for a fair in Paris, and many people visit it every year.”
“The Eiffel Tower was built in 1889 for a fair in Paris, and many people visit it every year.”

Can you fix this sentence for grammar and make it clear, using simple words?
“I always get nervous when I have to say something in English in class, so I usually practice it many times at home first.”
"I feel nervous when I need to speak English in class, so I practice many times at home first."

Come up with a better sentence for clarity, accuracy, and the English grammar for sentence below:
“I said I like to buy chains and earrings, but that was just an example sentence for practice. In real life, I don’t really buy chains or earrings, and I don’t need jewelry to catch people’s eyes.”
Use simple words please.
"I said I like buying chains and earrings, but that was only an example for practice. In real life, I don’t really buy chains or earrings, and I don’t need jewelry to get people’s attention."
